headline: MARKET WRAP — Jul 1
telegramText: MARKET WRAP — Jul 1, 2026 Built for autonomous systems. Useful for humans. DAILY SYNTHESIS Today's session was defined by a clear rotation away from semiconductors and crypto-linked equities toward financials, defense, and communication services — a quarter-end rebalancing signal as much as a regime shift. The persistent and accelerating weekly ETF outflows from crypto products add a structural overhang that cannot be dismissed as noise. Tomorrow's setup hinges on whether the selling pressure in high-multiple tech stabilizes or extends, with cyclicals and value poised to remain in favor if it does not. CLOSING PRICES SPY +0.6% | QQQ +0.2% | VIX +0.9% | TLT -2.2% | DXY +0.2% | OIL -2.0% vs MORNING SPY -1.8% | QQQ -4.1% | VIX +7.7% | TLT -1.1% | DXY flat | OIL -0.6% EVENING NEWS ▲ Gold rises as traders react to less hawkish comments from Fed Chair Kevin Warsh ▲ Fed’s Warsh eases inflation fears amid resilient US economy ▲ Tottenham Hotspur agrees deal worth up to £100M for Sandro Tonali in club-record transfer ▼ US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal, opting for annual reviews instead SCENARIO SHIFTS ↑ Late Cycle · ↑ Recession · ↑ Goldilocks · ↑ Stagflation · ↑ Hegemony · ↑ Displacement REGIME CHECK Risk Gauge: 100.0/100 (Critical). Phase: EXPANSION. SECTOR CLOSE Top sectors: ITA +2.0%, XLF +2.0%, XLC +1.7%, SMH -1.8%, XLU -2.7%. Sector dispersion: 64 (HIGH, breadth SELECTIVE_STRENGTH), 69% positive 1w.
